settings.address: 320 Lexington Ave, Suite 1200, New York, NY 10016

settings.title: Office Hours
settings.text:
Monday — Friday: 9:00 AM – 6:00 PM
Saturday: By appointment
Sunday: Closed

settings.question: What should I bring to my first consultation?
settings.answer: Bring any documents related to your matter — contracts, correspondence, court papers, or financial records. The more context we have, the better we can assess your situation. Don't worry if you're not sure what's relevant; we'll guide you through it.

settings.question: How are your fees structured?
settings.answer: Most matters are billed hourly with detailed monthly invoices. We also offer flat fees for estate planning, real estate closings, and certain business formation services. We always discuss fees openly before any engagement begins.

settings.question: How long does a typical case take?
settings.answer: Timelines vary widely depending on the nature and complexity of the matter. A simple real estate closing may take weeks, while complex litigation can take months or longer. We provide realistic time estimates during your initial consultation.

settings.question: Do you offer payment plans?
settings.answer: Yes. For qualifying matters, we can arrange structured payment plans. We believe cost should not be a barrier to quality legal representation. Ask about payment options during your consultation.

settings.question: What areas of New York do you serve?
settings.answer: Our office is in Midtown Manhattan, but we represent clients throughout New York City, Long Island, Westchester, and the greater tri-state area including New Jersey and Connecticut.
